commit.message:
docs: Add VMWare Workstation 25H2 docs, chore: restructure content (#41)

## Summary

Global restructuring and additions of content. Solved a few bugs, added
a new section about running VMWare Workstation 25H2 on Linux. Corrected
some typo's, moved content. etc.

## Type of change

<!-- Check all that apply -->

- [x] `fix` — bug fix (broken link, incorrect command, layout issue)
- [x] `content` — update or improve existing content
- [x] `docs` — changes to CONTRIBUTING, README, or meta documentation
- [x] `chore` — maintenance (dependencies, config, CI/CD)

> [PR title and commit types must follow these standards — view the
contributing
guide](https://github.com/Stensel8/Zephyrus-Linux/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#commit-messages)

## Checklist

- [x] PR title follows the commit convention (e.g. `fix: correct nmcli
command in eduroam guide`)
- [x] Both EN and NL versions updated (if applicable)
- [x] Media is in AVIF format (not PNG/JPG)
- [x] No broken image references (`/images/*.avif` all exist in
`static/images/`)
- [x] Tested locally with `hugo server`

---------

commit.message:
fix: swap mssql/server for azure-sql-edge on ARM64 hosts

The Azure Service Bus emulator depends on SQL Server, which has no ARM64
image. On Apple Silicon (and other ARM64 hosts), the mssql/server:2022-latest
container fails under QEMU with jemalloc address mapping errors.

Use Aspire's BeforeStartEvent to intercept the container model and swap the
image to azure-sql-edge:latest, which has native ARM64 support. The Service
Bus emulator works with either SQL engine.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add .http files for eShop REST API

Structured by bounded context with auth placeholders:
- catalog.http  — 5 endpoints (brands, types, items, item by id, update price)
- basket.http   — 4 endpoints (get, update, checkout, delete)
- ordering.http — 5 endpoints (list, get by id, create, cancel, ship)
- _variables.http — shared IDs and health check
- http-client.env.json — dev/aspire environment config

Authorization headers are commented out, ready to uncomment when auth is added.

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: agbishop <1376376+agbishop@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: agbishop <andrew.bishop9625@gmail.com>

fix(logics): update pyavd API compatibility for v6.x (#68)

* fix(generator): update pyavd API usage for compatibility with pyavd 6.x

pyavd 6.0.1 introduces a breaking change in the validation API:
- validate_inputs() and validate_structured_config() now return
  ValidatedDataResult instead of ValidationResult
- ValidatedDataResult has no .failed attribute; use .validated_data is None
- Violations are accessed via .validation_result.violations (not .validation_errors)
- Deprecations are accessed via .validation_result.deprecations

Also bump pyavd dependency from 5.7.2 to 6.0.1 and regenerate uv.lock.

Closes #67
Supersedes #59

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(logics): fix remaining pyavd 6.x API compatibility issues

- Replace removed 'name_servers' with 'dns_settings.servers' in example
  inventories (AVD 6.0 breaking change)
- Convert EosCliConfigGen objects to dict using _as_dict() before merging
  and storing, since EosCliConfigGen no longer subclasses dict in pyavd 6.x
- Replace removed private pyavd.api._anta.get_minimal_structured_configs()
  with public pyavd.api.anta.AVDFabricData.from_structured_configs() API
- Update get_device_test_catalog() call to use fabric_data= parameter

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(logics): broaden exception catch in ANTA catalog generation

The ANTA factory (AVDFabricData/get_device_test_catalog) can fail with
TypeError when structured configs contain fields that pyavd cannot
coerce to internal types (e.g. in test mocks that return plain dicts
with string platform values).

Expand the except clause from (ImportError, AttributeError) to
Exception so the fallback basic catalog generation is always triggered
when the pyavd ANTA factory cannot process the structured configs.

Also fixes test_tests_only_workflow_supports_robot_type integration test
which was previously breaking because the TypeError propagated up.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Phase 20: UI polish — profile API, toast, skeletons, improved UX

Backend:
- Add PATCH /auth/profile (update name, phone, avatar)
- Add PATCH /auth/password (change password with current password verification)

commit.message:
fix: use DIRECT_DATABASE_URL for migrations to avoid Neon pooler timeout

Neon's connection pooler doesn't support pg_advisory_lock needed by
Prisma migrate. Use direct (non-pooled) URL for migrations, fallback
to DATABASE_URL if not set.

commit.message:
fix(supervisor): handle worker failure with lease reclaim and task recovery

Previously, the supervisor sent the terminal heartbeat (→ COMPLETING)
BEFORE checking the finalization result. COMPLETING is a terminal lease
state with no path to CRASHED or RECLAIMED, so failed workers left the
lease permanently stuck and the task stranded in ASSIGNED.

Now the supervisor checks the finalization outcome first:
- Success: sends terminal heartbeat → COMPLETING (unchanged)
- Failure: calls LeaseReclaimService to atomically transition the lease
  to CRASHED → RECLAIMED, evaluate retry/escalation policy, and recover
  the task (ASSIGNED → READY or FAILED based on retry budget)

The catch block (for workspace/runtime exceptions) also attempts lease
reclaim as best-effort recovery.

Adds LeaseReclaimerPort to WorkerSupervisorDependencies and
createReclaimUnitOfWork to the application adapters.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(domain): add STARTING→COMPLETING lease transition for fast workers

Fast-completing workers (~1s) emit zero heartbeat events, so the lease
never reaches RUNNING. Previously this was worked around with a synthetic
'bridge' heartbeat that forced STARTING→RUNNING before the terminal
heartbeat. Three independent model critiques (Opus, GPT-5.4, Gemini 3)
identified this as a semantic lie and a staleness detection gap.

The real fix: add STARTING→COMPLETING as a valid state machine edge.
A worker that completes instantly genuinely skips RUNNING — the state
machine should reflect this reality. The terminal heartbeat still goes
through the heartbeat service (which sets heartbeatAt and validates the
transition), preserving staleness detection and audit trail integrity.

Removes the bridge heartbeat hack entirely.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
